content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm Weekly Report (Apr 19 - 25, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The development focus for the week of April 19–25, 2026, centered on expanding the cross-chain capabilities of the `plugin-evm` framework. The team successfully integrated support for the Radius Network, ensuring parity across both Rust and Python implementations. This update reinforces the project's commitment to modularity and broad network compatibility within the EVM ecosystem.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Radius Network Integration
The primary technical achievement this week was the integration of the Radius Network into the plugin, facilitating broader chain support for AI agents. 
* **Network Expansion:** Added support for the Radius Network (chain ID `723487`) and its corresponding testnet (chain ID `72344`).
* **Implementation Parity:** Updates were applied concurrently to both the Rust and Python codebases to maintain consistent chain configuration across the framework.
* **Documentation and Testing:** The integration included comprehensive updates to `README.md`, type definitions in `python/elizaos_plugin_evm/types.py` and `rust/src/types.rs`, and corresponding updates to integration tests to ensure stability. This work was completed via [#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/pull/29).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
* **Closed Issues:** No issues were formally closed during this reporting period.
* **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened, and there are currently no active issues tracked in the repository. The project remains in a stable state following the recent network integration.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Development activity this week was focused on the successful execution of the Radius Network integration. The work demonstrated a coordinated effort to maintain cross-language consistency, as evidenced by the simultaneous updates to both the Rust and Python components of the plugin. There were no significant discussions or external contributions noted during this period, indicating a focused internal development cycle.
